title: Chapter Seventy-Four: The Confession
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

Seven days and nights passed.

He focused on recuperation in the Radiant Demon Wing hall. Ate. Meditated. Circulated qi through weakened meridians. And every time he closed his eyes, the faces of Zhu Lianting and the Ma family elders filled his mind.

Their schemes were not shallow. They had picked a fight of a nature he had never experienced. Those of high authority putting all their effort into entangling him. It was a big problem.

*What should I do.*

He lay on his bed lost in thought. Originally he had intended to pick a fight and become an enemy they could not associate with. He thought it would be enough if he experienced prestigious family martial arts in the process. Not now. Their sophistication far surpassed him. They had an attitude of accepting whatever happened. A completely different kind of conflict.

The time he would get entangled going forward would be far greater than any support gained from accepting the Desolate Ma family.

He briefly considered revealing his constitution and lifespan to his grandfather.

*I absolutely cannot trust them.*

He dismissed it immediately. He grew up as a non-existent child in the Jeong Family estate. His maternal family did not reach out. That was how his growing years were.

After stepping into the murim, he saw the Hwangbo family's conduct -- wielding power for self-interest while claiming to be a pillar of orthodox martial arts. The Namgung family, called orthodox world's number one, plotted a borrowed knife murder against the man who had helped them. In the Blood Flame Sect he saw the bottom of humanity. Just an abyss.

Nothing but distrust.

*Aiming for Desolate Sect Elder Council Lord?* His grandfather was one who craved power. Even after cutting ties with his daughter, he tried to treat her son as grandson. He did it seeing usefulness. Who knew how he would shake Yeon-shin after grasping his weakness.

*Should I see the Sect Lord? Is it okay to create debt instead? I need to build merit.*

The sixteen-year-old young master agonized. Swoosh. He burrowed into his bedding. Even the hazily shining sunset was dazzling now.

The Radiant Demon Wing room was indeed his sanctuary. He could put down a warrior's spirit without worrying about gazes. Did not have to forcefully ignore his life with a set time limit.

He slowly curled up into a ball. As always. He hazily felt the orange edge of sunlight. The candle burning at both ends, except he was the candle, and the flame was everything he still needed to accomplish.

How long had passed like that?

"Young Hero Jeong! Young Hero Jeong!"

A familiar voice stimulated his consciousness. Won-chang. And behind him, tumultuous energy waves surging toward the Radiant Demon Wing hall. The presences of many seniors. Those who had first sent off the Sect Lord and Yeon-shin from the Blood Flame Sect headquarters had returned.

Creak.

Three people came in right away. Won-chang, Cheong Myeong, Baek Mi-ryeo. Yeon-shin, already risen, smiled faintly. They were companions who had come to save him all the way to the Blood Flame Sect main hall. He welcomed them gratefully.

Won-chang stared at him. Something in Yeon-shin's face must have shown. The residue of seven days spent arguing with himself, the loneliness of it.

"Did something happen?"

Yeon-shin looked at them. Slowly parted his lips.

What kind of wind was it? The thought occurred that if this was a life where he could not even trust these people, there was no reason to be bound by the fruit either.

It was fine without wine cups. The tranquil evening air substituted for wine's fragrance and side dishes.

He spoke a few words. Cheong Myeong and Baek Mi-ryeo raised large inner power barriers immediately. Energy screens. Sound could not leak out.

And Yeon-shin spoke.

He talked about family matters. He revealed his constitution -- the defective upper dantian, the burning meridians, the deterioration that could not be reversed. The sword song's cost: fifteen days of lifespan for every use, drawn from a total that had been short to begin with.

Three years and six months remaining. Perhaps less.

He told them about the Ma family. About Zhu Lianting's web. About the Grand Administrator's warning. About the suffocating weight of politics pressing against a life that had no room for delay.

He told them everything. It poured out of him like water from a broken vessel -- all the things he had carried alone since he first understood what his body was, what it could not do, how little time remained to do the rest. He spoke of his mother, who he had never known. Of the countryside martial house where he had been a ghost. Of the fruit that might save him, if it existed, if he could reach it, if he could earn it before the candle burned to nothing.

When he finished, silence.

The sunset poured through the window, painting all four of them in amber and shadow. Cheong Myeong's expression had emptied. The careful blankness of a man who had seen too much of the world to be surprised by cruelty, but who could still be wounded by the suffering of someone he cared for. Baek Mi-ryeo's face was the same -- still as carved stone, because showing her grief would be a burden she refused to place on him.

Hyeon Won-chang wept.

Silently at first. Then the silence broke. A single, wracking sniff. Tears rolled down his face -- the face of the Desolate Divine Hero, the man who wore his heart on his sleeve and made no apology for it. He wiped his eyes with white sleeves.

Then he straightened. Drew a breath. And when he spoke, his voice was rough but steady.

"Well. Let's first visit the Ma family. I thought of a decent plan."

"What?"

"Listen."

Won-chang leaned close and whispered for quite a while. Even though the two blue-ranked masters' energy screen was firm. It seemed like he was plotting a scheme.

Listening to it, that was exactly what it was.

Cheong Myeong patted Yeon-shin's shoulder. A single touch -- the Elf's way. Seemingly warm, but carrying more weight than any embrace.

Baek Mi-ryeo held him from behind. Brief. Fierce. Wordless. Her arms around him for three heartbeats, and then she let go.

No more words were needed. The concern of companions who shared life and death. The understanding of masters who made blades their friends.

Yeon-shin shook his head, embarrassed. For a moment he truly felt like the youngest. He was the youngest.

"Let's go!" Won-chang wiped his eyes with white sleeves and stood. The other three rose with him.

---

They arrived at the Desolate Ma family's residence at dusk. Two gatekeepers came forward.

"Please state your identity and --"

"No." Won-chang ignored the gatekeepers and shouted at the darkening sky. Inner power loaded into his voice, his tremendous shout shaking the dusky night air.

"LIANTING!"

The name echoed off stone walls and tile rooftops, rolling across the main sect's compounds like thunder. They said few outside knew Lady Zhu's origins. There must be private circumstances. But it was not something Radiant Demon Wing masters needed to consider.

"She's under the Sect Lord anyway," Cheong Myeong said with his sly smile, kicking lightly off the ground. "When even the crown prince trembles, what's a married branch imperial member..."

"LIANTING!"

"What is this!"

"Do you know who you're calling?"

The gatekeepers ran over with bewildered faces. Won-chang displayed traceless movement technique -- brushing off their hands, still shouting the name with casual disrespect that would spark duels elsewhere. An untimely drama unfolded in the night.

Yeon-shin slightly twisted his body and turned his head as if not part of the group.

*This is the plan. Let them make the scene. Let it draw witnesses.*

And witnesses came. People gathered, drawn by the disturbance. Desolate Sect was a cluster of various hall groups. Very many staying there. The night's commotion was worth watching. Dozens lined the courtyard in the growing dark.

---
